sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to be strong, like Syr. and Chald. ܚܣܢ, תֲסַן. Hence חָסֹון, חֲסִין, חֵסֶן. [“The primary idea lies in binding together; comp. in חָזַק No. 3.”]

(2) to be wealthy (see חֹסֶן ); whence to heap up, to lay up in store. (Arabic خزن, whence مَخْزَنُ a storehouse.)

Niphal, to be laid up, Isa 23:18.

Derivatives, see Kal No. 1.
